title: Studio Ghibli and episode 11
wikitext:
[]: Studio Ghibli and episode 11             Quote Post by V » Thu Jun 19, 2008 7:51 pm what exactly does it mean that Studio Ghibli "did the animation for episode 11: the Day Tokyo-3 Stood Still"? I mean was it that one or two prominent "Animation Directors" from Ghibli did some of the background work? Or did the ENTIRE episode get shipped off to Studio Ghibli to do the animation? Does it mean 100% of the animation in that episode was done by Ghibli?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jun 19, 2008 8:11 pm Jeez, you could look up the credits for Episode 11 in an internet search, or just PM someone in the know. (I don't know who the contractor was- but I'm sure the info's out there) That being said, thread locked.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Jun 19, 2008 8:58 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Jeez, you could look up the credits for Episode 11 in an internet search, or just PM someone in the know. (I don't know who the contractor was- but I'm sure the info's out there) That being said, thread locked.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 19, 2008 9:19 pm Even the episode commentary from the Renewal/Platinum booklet didn't say anything useful. The Eva Mailing List people might be able to tackle this one, actually.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Jun 19, 2008 9:23 pm Thank you, Ornette. Yes that is exactly what I mean: I am aware of the credits etc. but I am unsure of the exact relationship between Ghibli and this episode. (i.e. did just "a guy" who also is a prominent Ghibli member work on this one, or did the entire "Ghibli production studio" do it? For that matter...did they just like, work on the backgrounds or minor animation, or did they animate everything, including hero shots of major characters? ...the goal of an "Evangelion discussion forums" is to private message people in the hopes that they'll know what it is? I didn't know that was Standard Operating Procedure; thanks for clarifying that. Further, what about Production I.G.'s involvement in episodes 13 and 18? But yeah, episode 11 always stuck out for me as that episode where "everyone looks slightly different", even before I knew about the production issues. Thus it was very interesting when my suspicion was confirmed and it turned out Studio Ghibli had worked on it. All of the characters looks a little....different, though this may be because they're mostly appearing in darkness with heavy shadowing of the characters. Plus the election-truck lady looks like she's right out of a Ghibli movie. Reichu wrote: Even the episode commentary from the Renewal/Platinum booklet didn't say anything useful. The Eva Mailing List people might be able to tackle this one, actually.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 19, 2008 9:35 pm There are a few episodes where the characters don't look quite right. Check out episodes 04, 06, and 10, for example. And I'm serious about the EML. They were able to answer a question about the contributions of some animation studio (I think?) rather more obscure than Ghibli. Also, some stuff from the Platinum booklets. I'd recommend snagging them off Monkey's site and referencing them yourself as needed, assuming you haven't.... Not very useful, but it's something, at least. Episode 11 The animation for this episode was handled by Studio Ghibli, known for their work on such films as My Neighbor Totoro. Nozomu (written as "peek") Takahashi, the city assembly electoral candidate, who only appears as a name is a twist on the producer of Ghibli, Nozomu (written as "aspire") Takahashi. [Blooper here, since the name of the guy in the episode is pronounced "Nozoku".] Episode 13 The animation was done by Production I.G., known for their work on such projects as Blue Seed and Mobile Police Patlabor 2 the Movie. The cool visuals that they were known for fit this episode dealing with computers. Episode 18 This is the next episode Production I.G. cooperated or the production after Episode Thirteen "Angel Invasion”. Tensai Qkamura, who handled the storyboards and directing, has worked as a director on such shows as Medabots and the Stink Bomb segment of Memories, and among his more recent works is Wolf's Rain. Kazuya Kise is the star animator at Production I.G. Among his representative works are the theatrical Mobile Police Patlabor, Ghost in the Shell, and Blood, the Last Vampire.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Fri Jun 20, 2008 3:05 am To quote myself from that other thread: It's not the first time I hear this either, but I don't get why Ghibli always gets so much flak for being influenced by their own style for the characters, while everyone's apparently fine with the dork faces of... well, I'm not sure who was responsible for Eps 4, 10 & 12, but whenever I see those noses I feel like punching them in... And the quality is hardly consistent otherwise either. So why (only) the Ep 11 hate?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Jun 20, 2008 5:57 am Oops. I made a big boo-boo here. :duh-me: V, I'm sorry. My mistake.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Jun 20, 2008 10:46 am JimmyWolk wrote: tbh, 11 never stood out for me that much the first time I saw it, seeing how inconsistent the episodes were anyway. I only got a déjà vu at Asuka's grin when she makes Shinji open the door. That was so typical Ghibli.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jun 20, 2008 11:48 am All of episode 06 looks like crap.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Fri Jun 20, 2008 1:06 pm V wrote: JimmyWolk wrote: tbh, 11 never stood out for me that much the first time I saw it, seeing how inconsistent the episodes were anyway. I only got a déjà vu at Asuka's grin when she makes Shinji open the door. That was so typical Ghibli.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Jun 20, 2008 3:58 pm yeah I'll have to rewatch some Ghibli stuff to get a more concrete set of comparisons, but rewatching ep 11 right now, the scene in the elevator when the AC breaks down and its hot and Kaji suggests Misato take her shirt off and she gets embarrassed, her "embarrassed look" is *straight out of* a Ghibli movie.
